discussion_comments.comment_body: Resolved in 094af72d: buildCopilotArgs() now removes deny-listed tools from the allow set before emitting args, so deny wins when allow/deny overlap. Added a regression test for the overlap case.
Resolved in 094af72d: provider argv warning scan now includes --allow-all-urls, including when supplied via extraArgs. Added regression coverage for the warning.

Resolved: vendor and autodetect tiers now validate via isExecutableFile() rather than just existence, matching the env/config tiers. Updated all five affected tests to spy on isExecutableFile instead of fileExists.

Resolved: added `basename`, `isAbsolute` imports and a name-only contract check before any path join. Names that are absolute paths, contain path separators (`/`, `\`), equal `.` or `..`, or differ from their own `basename` are pushed to `missing[]` using the original `rawName` (so the caller's warning shows what the user actually wrote) rather than silently dropped. Leading/trailing whitespace is now trimmed before validation and deduplication.

Skipping. The resolved path is the load-bearing piece of debug info for "which Copilot binary did Archon pick up?" — without it, the `source: vendor|env|config|path` label tells you the *tier* but not which CLI actually ran (someone could have multiple installs on PATH). Username-in-path is the weakest tier of PII, and the rest of the codebase already logs paths (worktree paths, repo paths, `cwd`, etc.) at info level. The CLAUDE.md rule is aimed at tokens / secrets / user message content, not `~/Users/foo/.archon/...`. Happy to revisit if Pino structured logs ever become a shared / shipped surface.
